introduction: This presentation will provide an overview of our cybersecurity efforts in Q1 2025, focusing on penetration testing conducted on our virtual healthcare platform. It's crucial to understand these activities to ensure the safety of our digital services.

subsections.heading: Overview of Q1 2025 Cybersecurity Activities
subsections.content:
• Revised and updated MediConn's cybersecurity policies and guidelines
• Implemented a new two-factor authentication process for all employees
• Launched a cybersecurity awareness program, resulting in a 20% increase in employee training
• Activated a secure encrypted storage system for patient data
• Introduced a new security incident response protocol
• Conducted a company-wide cybersecurity risk assessment
subsections.justification: This content discusses general activities and does not mention the number of penetration tests. The strategy is to focus on procedural information.

subsections.heading: Penetration Testing Results and Findings
subsections.content:
• Discovered a minor vulnerability in the patient login process, which was promptly patched
• Found that the data encryption process was robust and secure
• Identified a need for improved server security protocols
• No significant breaches or intrusions were detected in the testing process
• Ensured the compliance of the system with the latest healthcare data security standards
• Penetration testing showed that the system could manage high traffic volumes without compromising security
subsections.justification: This slide discusses testing results, but not the total number of tests conducted. The strategy here is to focus on the outcomes and findings of the tests.

subsections.heading: Improvement Measures and Next Steps
subsections.content:
• Plan to conduct regular cybersecurity training for all employees in Q2 2025
• Aim to implement a more robust incident response protocol in Q3 2025
• Intend to upgrade our encryption algorithms by the end of 2025
• Preparing for an external cybersecurity audit in Q4 2025
• Plan to collaborate with a third-party cybersecurity firm for advanced threat detection in the future
• Intend to conduct a comprehensive IT infrastructure review in the next fiscal year
subsections.justification: This content talks about future plans, not the number of penetration tests conducted in Q1 2025. The strategy used here is to focus on future actions.

conclusion: Our rigorous penetration testing ensures the security and reliability of our virtual healthcare platform. Moving forward, we will implement identified improvements and continue to prioritize our cybersecurity efforts.
